text: {"extracted_facts": {"Student": "Demo Student", "Matric No": "WIA230123", "Faculty": "FSKTM", "Programme": "Software Engineering", "Year": 3, "CGPA": 3.10, "Citizenship": "Malaysian", "Bumiputera": "No", "Family Income Tier": "B40 (approx. RM 3,800/month)", "Income Proof": "EPF statement uploaded (parsed with 88% confidence)", "Scholarships Applied": "Yayasan UM", "Motivation Letter": "Submitted (784 words)"}, "flags": [{"severity": "warn", "message": "CGPA 3.10 is below the standard Yayasan UM maintenance threshold of 3.30. Hardship justification is included in the motivation letter — committee should weigh family circumstances against the 0.20 CGPA gap."}, {"severity": "info", "message": "Student is B40 Malaysian non-Bumiputera — not eligible for MARA; Yayasan UM is the primary viable need-based pathway at UM."}, {"severity": "info", "message": "Income proof is an EPF statement (not a payslip). Parsed value: RM 3,800/month. Within B40 band (< RM 4,850)."}], "recommendation": "approve", "reasoning": "Student meets income-based eligibility (B40) and is the primary need-based applicant profile Yayasan UM is designed for. The CGPA shortfall (3.10 vs 3.30 threshold) is real but within the hardship-consideration range; the motivation letter specifically cites family financial strain as the cause of the CGPA dip. EPF-based income proof is valid per Yayasan UM's accepted document list. Recommend approval with a 6-month CGPA review clause — if the student's CGPA does not recover to 3.30 by end of next semester, scholarship is flagged for review (not immediate revocation). This preserves the safety net while holding the student accountable for academic recovery."}
